name: Petronas Towers
summary: The Petronas Towers are twin skyscrapers in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, standing at 451.9 meters tall. They were the tallest buildings in the world from 1998 to 2004 and remain the tallest twin towers globally. Designed by Argentine architect Cesar Pelli, the towers symbolize Malaysia's rapid economic development and modernization.

timeline.title: Design Competition
timeline.description: Malaysian Prime Minister Mahathir Mohamad initiated the design competition for the towers as part of his vision to transform Kuala Lumpur into a global city. Argentine-American architect Cesar Pelli won the competition with his postmodern design inspired by Islamic geometric patterns.

timeline.title: Construction Begins
timeline.description: Construction of the Petronas Towers began with the foundation work. The towers required the world's deepest foundation at the time, with piles driven 120 meters into the ground due to the challenging soil conditions.

timeline.title: Structural Completion
timeline.description: The structural steelwork of both towers was completed. The towers feature a unique structural system with a reinforced concrete core and steel perimeter columns, designed to withstand high winds and seismic activity.

timeline.title: Official Opening
timeline.description: The Petronas Towers were officially opened by Prime Minister Mahathir Mohamad on August 31, 1998, coinciding with Malaysia's National Day. The towers became the world's tallest buildings at 451.9 meters, surpassing the Sears Tower in Chicago.

timeline.title: Loss of Tallest Building Title
timeline.description: The Petronas Towers lost their title as the world's tallest buildings to Taipei 101 in Taiwan. However, they remained the tallest twin towers in the world, a record they still hold today.

timeline.title: 20th Anniversary
timeline.description: The Petronas Towers celebrated their 20th anniversary, having become an iconic symbol of Kuala Lumpur and Malaysia. The towers attract over 1.5 million visitors annually to their skybridge and observation deck.
